headline_theme: AI in Product Management: Separating Hype from Reality
editorial:
Notion shipped its terminal-first agent build flow this week, and Lenny's interview with Max Schoening makes a quiet claim that should land louder: agency is now the bottleneck, not skill. The PMs you envy in 2026 are not the ones who learned six AI tools - they're the ones willing to ship a half-broken prototype on a Tuesday afternoon. 

In a world where AI is increasingly capable, the difference between good and great product managers is no longer about skills, but about agency. Agency is the ability to take action, to make decisions, and to drive change. It's the ability to cut through the noise, to prioritize, and to focus on what really matters. As Max Schoening noted in his interview, Notion's design and product lead has been effective in getting designers and PMs to ship code, prototype in the terminal, and launch extremely successful AI products. 

So what does this mean for product managers? It means that we need to focus on building our agency, not just our skills. We need to be willing to take risks, to experiment, and to learn from our failures. We need to be able to navigate the complexities of our organizations, to build alliances, and to drive change. As seen in the Notion AI agent build flow, we can use AI to automate tasks and free up time for more strategic work. By doing so, we can increase our agency and become more effective product managers. In short, we need to be able to get things done, even when it's hard. And that's what separates the good product managers from the great ones.
key_takeaways: Agency is now the bottleneck, not skill
The ability to take action and drive change is key to success in product management
Product managers need to focus on building their agency, not just their skills

must_reads.title: Why cultivating agency matters more than cultivating skills in the AI era
must_reads.why: I appreciate how this article emphasizes the importance of agency in product management, and how it's becoming the key differentiator between good and great product managers. The article's focus on the role of agency in driving change and taking action resonates with me, and I think it's a crucial takeaway for product managers.

must_reads.title: Build To Learn FAQ
must_reads.why: The build-to-learn approach is a game-changer for product managers looking to build their agency. By focusing on learning and experimentation, PMs can develop the skills and confidence they need to drive change and take action.

must_reads.title: Building AI Employees for Hospitality: How AITropos Takes Orders Where Customers Already Are
must_reads.why: I think this article is a great example of how AI can be used to build agency in product management. The way AITropos uses AI to automate tasks and free up time for more strategic work is a valuable insight for product managers looking to improve their agency.

contrarian.note: This article challenges the idea that building agency in product management requires a lot of time and effort. It shows how Notion AI agents can be used to create custom workflows in just 10 minutes, which can help product managers build their agency quickly and efficiently.
contrarian.title: Million-Dollar Workflows in 10 Minutes

also_worth.title: Build Your Own Notion AI Agent
also_worth.why: This video provides a step-by-step guide on how to create a Notion AI agent, which can help product managers automate tasks and build their agency.

pm_homework: Take a risk and ship a half-broken prototype this week
Identify one area where you can build your agency, and take action on it
Read one article on building agency in product management, and apply one insight to your work.
